text: <par>Note the similarity between the Monpa forms for 'tail' and the Mru forms <latinform>ʃr̀dblquote ɛmyi³</latinform> (163260) and <latinform>ʃr̀dblquote imi³</latinform> (163261); the 'p' in the Monpa forms may be epenthetic.</par>
<par>Cf. Thai <latinform>nǎŋ</latinform> SKIN, check Monpa forms for skin.</par>
<par><emph>khi</emph>: cf. Gallong <latinform>aki</latinform> XX above. M1 = m or 208?; 2103 (or 2111?); <latinform>laŋ</latinform></par>
<par>M1: would expect 1390: how? (and cf. <latinform>sa-sum</latinform> below) M2: what to do if remove Etym. <latinform>kum</latinform> (per JAM)?</par>
<par>check cognacy of Monpa Cangluo Muotuo and Monpa Tilang forms for dream (second syllables)</par>

<par>Note the similarity between the Monpa forms for 'tail' and the Mru forms <latinform>ʃr̀dblquote ɛmyi³</latinform> (163260) and <latinform>ʃr̀dblquote imi³</latinform> (163261); the 'p' in the Monpa forms may be epenthetic.</par>
<par>First two syll = MOUTH. Third syll ≠ *tsam HAIR (see HAIR OF HEAD).</par>

<par>In this dialect of Monpa, the first two syllables mean 'belly'. Note the similarity of the second two syllables to Darang tæ∑£¡ bo≥£∞ 'center' (cf. also Bokar lo-po≥ 'center'). The last syllable looks like #2111 'belly; center'.</par>
<par><latinform>ju</latinform> = 208? or m? M2: 1384 etc. or s?</par>
<par>la¹³ means 'mountain range, ridge' in Tsangla (Motuo), so kʻai⁵⁵la¹³ could be a compound with 'mountain' or it could be a reflex of #5560 *(k-)la.</par>
